mechanism_narrative: A 5-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) in periods of sustained industrial growth.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims signals a strengthening labor market that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) in periods of sustained labor market growth.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) in periods of sustained monetary easing.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) in periods of sustained housing growth above 600k units.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply environment that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 5-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) in periods of sustained industrial decline.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims signals a weakening labor market that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) in periods of sustained labor market decline.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) in periods of sustained monetary tightening above 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) in periods of sustained demand growth.
